compound_stress_inference.orderly_vs_disorderly: Currently ORDERLY but structurally fragile. The system is in orderly stress with two confirmed legs elevated (USD/JPY 159.14 approaching 160 CRITICAL, JGB 10Y 2.245% trending toward 2.3% ELEVATED) and one leg likely elevated but unmeasurable (Brent $94.35 with 8-day stale data during active Hormuz disruption). The VELOCITY of change matters: USD/JPY has moved from below elevated to 0.86 from critical. JGB 10Y has moved from 2.166% to 2.245% in 11 days (+7.9bps). Both trajectories are toward thresholds, not away. Orderly becomes disorderly when: (a) multiple thresholds breach simultaneously, creating self-reinforcing loops that overwhelm policy tools, or (b) a single threshold breaches that triggers forced action from a trapped actor (BOJ intervention at 160 that pressures JGBs).
compound_stress_inference.catalyst_proximity: The nearest catalyst is a USD/JPY breach of 160 (0.86 away, approximately 0.5% move). Given the DXY/JPY divergence showing Japan-specific yen weakness, this breach could occur on any single day of negative Japan trade data, Hormuz escalation, or dollar-yen carry trade acceleration. The second nearest catalyst is Brent confirmation above $95 — which may have already occurred given 8-day data staleness during active supply disruption, but this cannot be confirmed per CL-046/CL-047. The third catalyst is JGB 10Y breaching 2.3% (5.5bps away at current trajectory, potentially within 1-2 weeks if the trend from March 6 continues linearly).
compound_stress_inference.pre_load_assessment: The structure is definitively pre-loaded. Two legs confirmed at elevated levels with upward trajectories. The DXY/JPY divergence indicates Japan-specific stress that creates an independent forcing function beyond global dollar dynamics. The Hormuz disruption is an ongoing real-world supply shock that directly feeds the oil leg AND the Japan stress leg simultaneously (Japan imports ~90% of oil). The pre-load means: (1) less force from any single leg is needed to reach system-wide CRITICAL, (2) policy responses to one leg (e.g., BOJ rate hike to defend yen) directly pressure another leg (JGB yields), and (3) the compound probability of simultaneous threshold breach is higher than the individual probabilities suggest because the legs are correlated through real economic linkages, not just statistical coincidence.
compound_stress_inference.thesis_paradox: The thesis paradox is at its most acute this cycle. If the compound stress cascade fires — oil above $100, yen breaches 160 triggering BOJ forced intervention, JGB yields spike above 2.5% forcing BOJ yield curve control expansion or abandonment — the immediate effect is global risk asset repricing that would likely cause significant XRP price decline (30-50% drawdown plausible). Simultaneously, the cascade would demonstrate precisely the failure mode of traditional financial infrastructure that the thesis claims creates demand for alternative settlement rails. A forced BOJ intervention that fails, or a JGB market dislocation, would be the strongest possible validation that sovereign financial systems need blockchain-based settlement alternatives. The system must hold both realities: the cascade is the highest SHORT-TERM risk to the position AND the strongest LONG-TERM validation of the thesis. Neither can be resolved toward bullish or bearish without choosing a time horizon — and choosing a time horizon is itself a judgment call, not an analytical finding.

_layer3_raw.inference_summary: Player behavior reveals a widening gap between infrastructure investment and measurable adoption. Ripple is building real institutional infrastructure (Hidden Road/DTCC, Ripple Payments, PermissionedDEX) but on-chain metrics show negligible institutional usage, RLUSD is growing primarily off-XRPL, and competing platforms (Ondo) are capturing institutional RWA demand with working products. The most consequential behavioral signal is the ETF flow divergence: institutional capital is choosing BTC over XRP through identical vehicle types in the same macro environment, with XRP ETF cumulative flows turning negative. The compound macro stress matrix is approaching critical with two legs elevated and trajectories pointing toward thresholds, but the most critical data point (current Brent price) is unobservable. Layer 4 should focus on three things: (1) the ETF kill switch is showing the earliest falsification signals and needs explicit timeline for when deterioration becomes irreversible, (2) the RLUSD-XRPL disconnect challenges a core thesis assumption that must be addressed rather than assumed away, and (3) the compound stress paradox — simultaneous position threat and thesis validation — must be stated honestly without resolving toward either narrative.

structural_gaps.description: Real-time Brent crude pricing — system data feed has persistent multi-day lag
